 5. Return true if any items are found, false otherwise.

 - Check if the result vector is not empty and return accordingly.

    `auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");`

    `conn | sqlgen::drop<Inventory> | sqlgen::if_exists;`

    We can use the literal operator `_c` to create a column object: `"quantity"_c > 50`.

    Then, we can chain the read operation with where and order_by:
    `auto result = (sqlgen::read<std::vector<Inventory>> | sqlgen::where("quantity"_c > 50) | sqlgen::order_by("quantity"_c.desc()))(conn);`

 5. Check the result and return.

 However, note that the `read` function returns a `Result` type (which may contain an error). We must handle the error.

```cpp
std::vector<SupProd> full_join_suppliers_products() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals; // for the column literals

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Build the query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Supplier>("name"_t1 | sqlgen::as<"supplier_name">)
               | sqlgen::full_join<Product>("supplier_id"_t1 == "supplier_id"_t2)
               | sqlgen::to<std::vector<SupProd>>;

  // Execute the query and return the result
  return query(conn);
}
```

```cpp
bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {

  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    if (!result) {

  }

}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
```

   namespace project_time_tracking {

     std::string name;

     int project_id;
     sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
     sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> end_time;
   };

   struct TimeMetrics {
     sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
     std::string name;
     int day;
     int month;
     int year;
     int hour;
     int minute;
     int second;
     double duration_days;
   };

   std::vector<TimeMetrics> project_time_tracking() {
     using namespace sqlgen::literals;

     auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
